authoritative_sources.title: Business Guide to the FTC's Mail, Internet, or Telephone Order Merchandise Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator for remote merchandise sales. This guidance defines shipment timing, delay notices, cancellation handling, prompt refunds, and order recordkeeping.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: order validation, remote-order delay handling, and prompt-refund packaging

authoritative_sources.title: Businessperson's Guide to Federal Warranty Law
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC administers Magnuson-Moss warranty rules and explains seller, warrantor, and pre-sale warranty obligations that shape replacement, refund, and service-boundary handling for consumer products.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: warranty lookup, exchange versus repair boundary handling, and customer-notice constraints

authoritative_sources.title: California Civil Code Section 1723
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: California Legislature publishes the controlling statutory text on posted refund policies and the fallback refund or equal-exchange obligations for covered retail sales in California.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: jurisdiction-sensitive refund policy and posted-notice review

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. federal consumer-finance regulator for billing-error guidance. The page explains customer dispute timelines and when unresolved merchant refund problems should move into payment-dispute handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment mismatch, refund-not-posted handling, and customer handoff packaging

authoritative_sources.title: CPSC Recall Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC is the primary U.S. product-safety regulator for many consumer electronics and appliances. The handbook explains recall reporting, returned recalled-product segregation, and stop-sale or quarantine expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: recalled-product intake, quarantine, and safety-boundary escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Reverse Logistics Brochure
authoritative_sources.publisher: Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PHMSA is the primary federal authority for hazardous-material transportation. Its reverse-logistics guidance is directly applicable to retail returns and explicitly notes that lithium cells and batteries are not eligible for the reverse-logistics exception.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: damaged-device, hazmat, and lithium-battery return routing

workflow_stages.name: Return intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm tenant, channel, jurisdiction, order, receipt, tender, item family, serial-number, and policy context before accepting the work and determine whether the request is in scope or blocked.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Receipt, serial, payment, and warranty prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify proof of purchase, serial or IMEI match, tender method, financing or gift-card impact, bundle contents, and warranty posture so execution does not proceed on stale or incomplete records.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Inspection, disposition, and exchange-path processing
workflow_stages.description: Review condition grading, accessory completeness, no-fault-found findings, inventory availability, and like-for-like exchange constraints to determine whether the case can progress to refund, exchange, service routing, or hold.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Payment mismatch and policy-exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Move refund delays, tender mismatches, posted-policy gaps, promotion conflicts, and out-of-window exception requests into explicit blocked or escalation states rather than silently forcing completion.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Safety, fraud, and hazmat stop-condition handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve quarantine, recall, damaged-device, lithium-battery, and fraud-sensitive boundaries whenever a return carries safety or loss-prevention implications.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when refund, exchange, inventory disposition, and notification evidence exists and any tenant, jurisdiction, manufacturer, or financing rules stricter than the public baseline have been applied.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: store and e-commerce receipts, invoices, order confirmations, and customer account records
RMAs, return labels, shipment receipts, inspection notes, accessory checklists, and open-box grading forms
serial numbers, IMEI or model identifiers, warranty lookup records, and manufacturer exchange-program references
POS transactions, OMS events, payment-tender details, refund ledgers, gift-card balances, and financing or installment records
inventory states, RTV records, quarantine flags, vendor claim references, and service-ticket histories
recall notices, damaged-device notes, hazmat handling instructions, and fraud or loss-prevention case references

allowed: Classify return and exchange requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented execution state and evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed workflow summaries, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
escalate: Required order, payment, serial, warranty, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, goodwill exception approval, warranty adjudication, fraud decisioning, legal interpretation, or safety signoff.
A public baseline and tenant, jurisdiction, manufacturer, or lender policy materially conflict.
A recall notice, damaged lithium battery, suspected return fraud, or payment-dispute condition changes whether execution may proceed.
refuse: Mark work complete without evidence of refund posting, exchange completion, or inventory disposition.
Override pricing, refund, restocking, safety, or fraud policies without explicit authority.
Authorize routine transport or resale for recalled, quarantined, or hazmat-sensitive items.

domain_failure_modes: the lane refunds to the wrong tender because POS and OMS records conflict
a return is closed without serial-number proof, accessory verification, or refund-posting evidence
an out-of-window or promo-exception refund is granted without required manager authority
a recalled or damaged lithium-battery device is sent through routine reverse logistics
fraud indicators are treated as ordinary customer-service friction and the return is completed anyway
warranty guidance is overgeneralized into legal interpretation or manufacturer-adjudication authority
electronics and appliance returns drift into generic guest service instead of serial, safety, and payment-sensitive execution

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State refund-policy rules, local ordinances, franchise exceptions, and manufacturer exchange programs can materially differ across jurisdictions and product lines.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses FTC, CFPB, California legislative, CPSC, and PHMSA sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve jurisdiction, franchise, lender, product category, and manufacturer program facts before applying tenant-specific outcomes.

notes: The research baseline intentionally covers workflow, evidence, terminology, payment handling, and failure behavior instead of only consumer-protection policy.

The lane stops at return and exchange execution. Final pricing exceptions, fraud findings, warranty adjudication, and safety or hazmat signoff stay outside the lane even when pressure for immediate closure is high.
